title: persona::update runs SELECT COUNT then UPDATE — two round-trips, TOCTOU
description: persona::update first does SELECT COUNT(*) FROM personas WHERE username = ? and checks count == 0 to validate existence, then runs a second UPDATE query. Between those two queries another actor could delete the persona; the UPDATE would silently affect 0 rows and the function would return Ok, printing 'Updated persona @X' even though nothing changed. The existence check is better expressed by checking rows_affected() on the UPDATE itself, or using RETURNING, eliminating the race and the extra round-trip.
close_reason: Fixed: eliminated TOCTOU by removing SELECT COUNT, using UPDATE + rows_affected.
